title: antennaria pulcherrima subsp. pulcherrima
intro: Antennaria pulcherrima subsp. pulcherrima, also known as the beautiful or purple-fruited antlerwort, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is a low-growing, perennial herb with yellow flowers and purple fruits, traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues. The plant is believed to possess medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can help to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems. Antennaria pulcherrima subsp. pulcherrima can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in salves and ointments to treat skin conditions and wounds.
